title: Bounded Promise Concurrency, Queueing, Ordering, Backpressure, and Cancellation · Contract Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for bounded promise concurrency, queueing, ordering, backpressure, and cancellation and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, compile diagnostics, explicit non-claims, and named browser, TypeScript, Node, network, filesystem, terminal, load, accessibility, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded abortable detail-prefetch scheduler. Learners progress through predic…
objectives: Justify prefetch from learner latency and API fair-use evidence and identify views where it must remain disabled.
Set active, queued, byte, request, time, and retry budgets independent of result count.
Track queued, active, settled, canceled, and published tasks with exactly one owner for scheduling and cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex Ts Prefetch Decision · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 9107 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce async queue and concurrency budgets, ordering, partial results, abort, and rejection ownership; unknown external values must not cross the runtime validator; then change one unknown JSON field, nested member, status, URL, or byte budget and re-run validation. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Justify prefetch from learner latency and API fair-use evidence and identify views where it must remain disabled.
